phases.title: Announcement / Writ Drop
phases.description: The election is officially called. Candidate nomination period opens. This marks the formal start of the electoral process.

phases.title: Campaign Period
phases.description: Political parties and candidates campaign. Debates, rallies, and media coverage intensify. This is your opportunity to research and decide.

phases.title: Voter Registration Deadline
phases.description: Last date to register to vote or update your enrolment details. Missing this date means you cannot vote in this election.

phases.title: Pre-Poll Silence Period
phases.description: Campaign blackout period. No new political advertising or campaigning allowed. Time to make your final decision in peace.

phases.title: Voting Day
phases.description: Polling booths are open. Eligible voters cast their ballots. This is the day your voice is heard.
voter_actions: Go to your registered polling booth
Cast your ballot — your vote matters

phases.title: Vote Counting
phases.description: Ballots are counted by trained electoral officials. Preliminary results begin to emerge. The process is transparent and verified.

phases.title: Official Results & Swearing-In
phases.description: Results are officially certified. Winning candidates are sworn into office. Democracy is complete — until the next election.
